data.body:
PASS 1 (G6 — approach soundness) review.

Verdict: substantially a PASS. The chosen approach — a verified fake whose contract tests run against BOTH the real and fake client — is the canonical SWE-at-Google pattern and is the right fit for the named problem (preventing fixture/fake drift that reintroduced bug 0ee6). I generated two structural alternatives: (a) a JSON-schema/OpenAPI-only validation of the fake's outputs with no real-client run, and (b) a golden-snapshot diff of fake vs. recorded real responses. Both are strictly weaker: (a) drops the live cross-check that is the whole point of a *verified* fake; (b) is the change-detector anti-pattern the plan explicitly rejects. The plan's choice is defensibly at-least-as-good on all axes — DISCARD.

Two grounded coaching findings (both minor; do not block):

CRITERION G6 (mechanism correctness / fitness-for-purpose):
- [ ] Make explicit WHEN the promised drift is actually caught. The fake returns story-A fixtures verbatim (`tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/_fakes.py` returns `copy.deepcopy` with no reshaping), and those fixtures were captured FROM the real client. So the fake cannot fail a *shape* contract via its own code — the only thing that surfaces "a future real-API change" is the live-gated run, which is opt-in and won't execute in the default suite. The plan should state that fixture-staleness detection depends on periodically running the live arm (and/or re-capturing fixtures), so reviewers don't expect default-CI to catch real-API drift.
  evidence: plan "a future real-API change surfaces as a fake-drift failure" vs. AC "Running against live is opt-in (does not flake or slow the default suite)"; `_fakes.py:53-62` returns fixtures with no shape massaging.

CRITERION G6 (mechanism correctness — concrete codebase fact):
- [ ] Name the ACTUAL pytest marker. The plan says "behind the existing live/external pytest marker", but no `live` marker is registered — `pyproject.toml:327` registers `external` (third-party live services) and the repo runs under `--strict-markers --strict-config` (`pyproject.toml:326`), so an unregistered `@pytest.mark.live` is a hard collection error. State that the live arm uses the existing `external` marker (and lives under `tests/external/`, per the `pytest_collection_modifyitems` confinement in `tests/conftest.py`).
  evidence: ABSENCE of a `live` marker in registered markers; `pyproject.toml:326-336`; `tests/conftest.py:63` external-tier confinement.
  suggested_fix: replace "behind the existing live/external pytest marker" with "behind the existing `external` marker (test resides under `tests/external/`)".

Affirmations (criteria this chunk passes): G6 mechanism is logic/data-flow complete for the four enrichment methods, all of which exist on the real surface (`search_issues`, `acli_graph.get_comment_map`/`get_issuelinks_map`/`get_parent_map`); the structure/type-not-value assertion design correctly avoids the change-detector anti-pattern; scope cleanly excludes story A/B; and the plan states a clear POSITIVE rationale ("A fake is only as good as its fidelity… verified fake… same tests run against BOTH").
